narrative: OMX30 opens Monday in a structurally bullish trend (+7.8% above 200-MA) but technical momentum is fading—MACD below signal, RSI neutral at 40.27, and critically, ADX is weak at 14.9, signaling low directional conviction. Global equity markets showed mixed performance with Europe split (DAX +0.05%, CAC -0.69%) and Asia soft (Hang Seng -0.87%), while the weighted correlation signal is slightly negative at -0.049%. Rising US 10Y yields (+0.60%) and elevated VIX (+4.54% to 17.97) create a seesaw: banks benefit from wider NIMs but real estate and tech face headwinds, while surging oil (+2.81%) and copper (+3.87%) lift mining but pressures industrials and consumer spending. UK political turmoil (Starmer under siege) adds ambient risk-off sentiment. Given weak ADX momentum, OMX30 likely consolidates near the 50-MA at 3180 with modest upside bias capped by technicals.
morningBrief: OMX30 traders: expect a quiet, range-bound session. The index is constructively positioned above the 200-MA (3123 pivot zone) but lacks momentum—MACD inverted, RSI midpoint, ADX weak. Watch support at 3085 (lower Bollinger Band) and resistance at 3180 (50-MA). Sector rotation: play Banks (SEB A, Handelsbanken A) for NIM expansion on 4.39% US yields, but avoid Real Estate (Balder, Castellum) on refinancing cost concerns. Industrials (Volvo B, Atlas Copco) face oil headwind but USD/SEK weakness (-0.71%) dulls export tailwind. Boliden is the outlier—copper +3.87%, gold +0.43%—consider it a contrarian long. NASDAQ flat (+0.39%) limits upside for Ericsson/Hexagon. US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET is the only catalyst; soft data could reignite risk-off. VIX at 17.97 signals moderate caution; respect the technicals.
drivers: Weak ADX (14.9) limits conviction; reduce magnitude by 30%
Mixed global signals: Europe mixed, Asia weak, US modest gains
Oil +2.81% and rising US yields (4.39%) create conflicting sector pressures
OMX30 firmly in bull regime (+7.8% vs 200-MA) but momentum softening (MACD below signal, RSI 40.27 neutral)

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield +0.60% to 4.39% widens NIM expectations; positive for Swedish credit-sensitive banks.
Rising US yields increase refinancing costs and cap property valuations via DCF compression.
Brent oil +2.81% to 104.1 increases input costs; partially offset by weak EUR/SEK but export tailwind muted.
Copper +3.87% and gold +0.43% directly boost Boliden revenue; commodity strength dominant driver.
NASDAQ +0.39% provides modest support, but rising yields create DCF headwind; net neutral.
Oil inflation pressures household spending; weak EUR/SEK increases H&M's sourcing costs.

watchList.reason: Copper +3.87%, gold +0.43%—exceptional commodity tailwind offsetting any macro weakness.
US yields +60bp supports NIM expansion; financials are beneficiaries in risk-neutral environment.
Oil inflation headwind outweighs modest USD/SEK benefit; industrials under pressure.
Tech sector flattish; lack of momentum in NASDAQ (+0.39%) limits upside despite defensive appeal.

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims 14:30 CET (consensus: monitor for surprise). No major Swedish macro data today.
topRisk: UK political crisis (Starmer under pressure) could trigger broader risk-off sentiment, spiking VIX above 20 and pushing OMX30 below support. US jobs data miss would amplify retreat.
technicalSummary: Momentum fading: MACD below signal (hist=-3.712), RSI 40.27 (neutral), ADX 14.9 (weak—confidence reduced 30%)
Volatility elevated: VIX +4.54% to 17.97; price in lower half of Bollinger bands (%B=0.35)
ROC-90 strong at +8.1% but short-term technicals deteriorating; expect consolidation

newsHeadlines.description: Greater Manchester mayor is the frontrunner to replace Keir Starmer, if he can secure a parliamentary seat

newsHeadlines.title: UK government bonds weaken as Starmer fights for premiership
